rag_rules: Place output range guarantees in component type scopes when concrete bounds or visible feature names are provided.
Use visible identifiers and named constants in AGREE contracts to ensure type alignment and semantic clarity.
generic_rules: Guarantees should reference valid component ports or visible variables with clear type definitions.
Assumptions and guarantees must align with the component's feature and property scope.
Use boolean expressions to define value ranges in AGREE contracts.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Declare range checks as boolean variables using 'eq' for clarity and reuse.
Ensure all AGREE annex blocks are syntactically valid with proper scoping.
anti_patterns: Avoid placing implementation-level bindings in component type contracts.
Do not reference subcomponent ports directly in type-level contracts.
